items.title: CEIGALL delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with 37% YoY revenue growth and 78% PAT growth, driven by strong EPC execution (margins expanded 633 bps...
items.description_html:
CEIGALL delivered exceptional Q4 FY26 results with 37% YoY revenue growth and 78% PAT growth, driven by strong EPC execution (margins expanded 633 bps) and Annuity Projects turnaround.

However, significant cash conversion concerns remain with negative operating cash flow of ₹912.83 Mn for FY26. Balance sheet strengthened with D/E ratio improving to 0.61x.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 37.06% YoY to ₹13,865.10 Mn in Q4 FY26

PAT grew 78.25% YoY to ₹1,290.34 Mn in Q4 FY26

EBITDA margin expanded 348 bps YoY to 16.12%

EPC segment results doubled with 112.5% YoY growth and margins expanded 633 bps to 14.38%

Annuity Projects segment turned profitable at ₹96.56 Mn vs loss in previous quarters

Debt-to-Equity ratio improved to 0.61x from 0.76x YoY

Risk Factors

Operating cash flow negative at ₹912.83 Mn for FY26 (CFO/PAT ratio -0.30x)

Working capital strain with over ₹14,300 Mn locked in current assets

Other Income declined 47% YoY to ₹123.02 Mn in Q4

Annuity Projects full year profitability declined 91.1% YoY

items.title: BHARAT FORGE Q4 FY26 showed mixed results with 17.5% revenue growth driven by Defence and Others segments offset by 17.5% PAT decline due to 38.9% raw...
items.description_html:
BHARAT FORGE Q4 FY26 showed mixed results with 17.5% revenue growth driven by Defence and Others segments offset by 17.5% PAT decline due to 38.9% raw material cost inflation and ₹987M exceptional losses.

The standalone entity recorded a loss of ₹1,178M after massive ₹5,000M impairment on e-mobility bet KPTL. Capex overspend of 128% vs guidance and working capital deterioration (₹7,090M receivables growth) raise execution concerns despite positive free cash flow of ₹346Cr.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 17.5% YoY to ₹45,280.43M driven by Defence (+46.4%) and Others (+157.1%) segments

Defence segment profit grew 130.6% YoY with margins expanding 178 bps to 4.9%

Free cash flow turned positive at ₹346.1Cr (FY26) vs negative in FY25 due to capex moderation

Finance costs declined 22.6% YoY for full year indicating debt reduction benefits

Defence order book stands at ₹9,467Cr providing long-term revenue visibility

Risk Factors

PAT declined 17.5% YoY to ₹2,325.65M due to 38.9% raw material cost spike and exceptional losses

EBITDA margin compressed 250 bps YoY to 15% as raw material costs grew faster than revenue

Standalone entity recorded ₹1,177.57M loss after ₹4,929.64M impairment on e-mobility investment

Trade receivables grew ₹7,090.42M causing 17.2% decline in operating cash flow

Capex overspent by 128% vs guidance (₹1,140.5Cr vs ₹500Cr target)

items.title: Emami’s acquisition of a 60% stake in IncNut values the 15-year-old company at Rs 535 crore. That is below the Rs 850 crore valuation it received in ...
items.description_html:
Emami’s acquisition of a 60% stake in IncNut values the 15-year-old company at Rs 535 crore.

That is below the Rs 850 crore valuation it received in 2022, when it raised funding from VentureEast, according to Tracxn. Other backers include RPSG Ventures.

Its revenues have declined from Rs 232 crore in FY23 to Rs 175 crore in FY25.

It will be interesting to see how IncNut ramps up its valuation for the remaining 40% stake in future tranches while working with Emami.


The Arc: 🚨 Personal and healthcare major Emami is acquiring 60% stake in the owner of D2C brands Vedix and Skinkraft for Rs 321 crore.

The remaining stake in IncNut, the parent company, will be acquired over four years based on performance.

items.title: MRF delivered strong YoY performance with revenue up 13.7% and PAT up 37.6%, driven by improved gross margins. However, sequential momentum weakened w...
items.description_html:
MRF delivered strong YoY performance with revenue up 13.7% and PAT up 37.6%, driven by improved gross margins. However, sequential momentum weakened with flat QoQ revenue and 218 bps QoQ margin compression due to input cost pressures. The company generated exceptional cash flow (CFO/PAT 1.89x) and strengthened its balance sheet significantly (net cash Rs 2,873 Cr, debt down 18.5%).

No management guidance was provided for comparison, so no guidance beats can be claimed despite the strong absolute performance.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 13.7% YoY to Rs 8,044.22 Cr in Q4; full-year revenue up 10.6% to Rs 31,149.01 Cr

PAT grew 37.6% YoY to Rs 702.25 Cr; full-year PAT up 29.5% to Rs 2,426.10 Cr

EBITDA margin expanded 103 bps YoY to 16.22% on lower raw material costs as % of sales

Operating cash flow more than doubled (146.2% YoY) to Rs 4,590.30 Cr with CFO/PAT at 1.89x

Free cash flow robust at Rs 3,156.05 Cr; balance sheet strengthened with net cash at Rs 2,873 Cr

Total debt reduced 18.5% YoY (Rs 537.85 Cr); working capital improved by Rs 1,961 Cr swing YoY

Risk Factors

Sequential revenue flat (-0.1% QoQ) indicating loss of momentum at year-end

EBITDA margin contracted 218 bps QoQ due to sequential spike in input costs (material cost up 6.5% QoQ)

Employee costs rose 17.0% YoY and other expenses up 20.9% YoY, partially offsetting gross margin gains

Management's prior outlook for muted CV and low single-digit PV growth was contradicted by 10.6% full-year growth

items.title: STYLAMIND delivered strong FY26 results with PAT growth of 23% YoY and EBITDA margin expansion of 148 bps to 19.54%, demonstrating operating leverage ...
items.description_html:
STYLAMIND delivered strong FY26 results with PAT growth of 23% YoY and EBITDA margin expansion of 148 bps to 19.54%, demonstrating operating leverage as revenue grew 10.2% while expenses grew only 7.5%. The company's near debt-free balance sheet and strong cash conversion (OCF/PAT 1.18x) support its capacity expansion plans, though the new plant has been delayed to June 2026.

Management's FY27 revenue guidance of ₹1,500-1,600 crore requires 33-42% growth, which appears achievable assuming the new plant delivers ₹300-400 crore as projected.

Positive Factors

PAT grew 23% YoY to ₹14,987.06 Lakh in FY26

EBITDA margin expanded 148 bps YoY to 19.54% in FY26

Operating cash flow grew 65.1% YoY to ₹17,741.42 Lakh
Net worth grew 22.8% YoY to ₹80,695.46 Lakh

Q4 EBITDA margin expanded 344 bps YoY to 19.63%
Export mix improved to 65% in Q3 indicating higher margin market share gain

Revenue grew 10.2% YoY to ₹1,129.29 Crore

Risk Factors

New plant commissioning delayed by ~9 months vs original September 2025 target

Employee benefits expense grew 30.7% YoY in Q4 indicating rising internal costs

US tariff uncertainty could impact export sales to US market

Export dependency on Europe over 50% of exports poses structural risk

Working capital days may lengthen due to 76.6% YoY surge in trade payables

items.title: INDOCO reported a strong Q4 FY26 with revenue growth of 18.8% YoY and EBITDA margin recovery to 11.7% (up 1192 bps YoY), demonstrating operational tur...
items.description_html:
INDOCO reported a strong Q4 FY26 with revenue growth of 18.8% YoY and EBITDA margin recovery to 11.7% (up 1192 bps YoY), demonstrating operational turnaround. International business was the star performer with 71.1% YoY growth, offsetting domestic weakness.

However, subsidiary losses of ₹52.6 Cr in Q4 continue to drag consolidated profitability despite strong standalone performance. Cash generation remains strong with positive FCF, but debt levels increased to ₹1,036 Cr raising financing cost concerns.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 18.8% YoY to ₹455.9 Cr

EBITDA turned positive at ₹53.5 Cr in Q4 vs loss of ₹0.8 Cr in Q4 FY25

EBITDA margin improved to 11.7% in Q4 from -0.2% YoY (1192 bps expansion)

International business revenue surged 71.1% YoY to ₹208.8 Cr in Q4

Operating cash flow strong at ₹192.2 Cr for FY26, with positive FCF of ₹44.7 Cr

International business full-year growth of 21.4% met management's 20%+ guidance

Risk Factors

Domestic business declined 5.6% YoY in Q4 to ₹247.1 Cr

Subsidiaries created PBT drag of ₹52.6 Cr in Q4, turning standalone profit into consolidated loss

Total debt increased to ₹1,036 Cr, finance costs up 155% YoY

Trade receivables grew 38.8% YoY, faster than revenue, indicating working capital stress

Full-year FY26 loss of ₹98.7 Cr despite strong cash generation

Auditor flagged material uncertainty regarding subsidiary FPP Holding LLC's ability to continue as going concern

items.title: CRAFTSMAN delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with revenue of ₹8,069 Crore exceeding the ₹7,000 Crore target by 15.3%, and EBITDA of ₹1,239 Cro...
items.description_html:
CRAFTSMAN delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with revenue of ₹8,069 Crore exceeding the ₹7,000 Crore target by 15.3%, and EBITDA of ₹1,239 Crore beating the ₹1,100 Crore target by 12.6%. PAT grew 97.4% YoY to ₹384 Crore with EBITDA margin expanding 218 bps to 16.12%.

All three segments showed margin expansion with Powertrain leading at 600 bps improvement. However, capex overspend of 48% above guidance and elevated leverage (Net Debt/EBITDA at 2.55x) are key concerns to monitor.

Positive Factors

Revenue beat guidance by 15.3% - ₹8,069 Crore vs ₹7,000 Crore target

EBITDA beat guidance by 12.6% - ₹1,239 Crore vs ₹1,100 Crore target

PAT grew 97.4% YoY to ₹384 Crore

EBITDA margin expanded 218 bps YoY to 16.12%

Powertrain EBIT margin expanded 600 bps YoY to 19.2%

Aluminium Products revenue grew 57.9% for full year FY26

Operating cash flow strong at ₹522 Crore with 1.36x CFO/PAT ratio

Subsidiaries contributed over 40% of group revenue and PAT

Risk Factors

Capex overspend of 48% above guidance - ₹1,188 Crore vs ₹750-800 Crore target

Net Debt to EBITDA at 2.55x above target of 2.0x

Free cash flow negative at ₹667 Crore due to heavy
capex

Working capital deteriorated with inventory build-up of ₹411 Crore

ETR increased significantly to 32.7% in Q4 from 11.6% in Q4 FY25

items.title: Interesting to see early Google backer Ram Shriram’s venture fund, Sherpalo, lead a major funding round in an Indian startup after over a decade. She...
items.description_html:
Interesting to see early Google backer Ram Shriram’s venture fund, Sherpalo, lead a major funding round in an Indian startup after over a decade.

Sherpalo was very active in India during the 2006–2010 period, backing companies like Info Edge, Cleartrip, and MapmyIndia.

It sold its India portfolio in 2014 to Lightbox, which was run by its former India head.

Since then, Sherpalo has participated in a few funding rounds but has not taken a lead role.

Sherpalo first backed Skyroot in 2022 and is now doubling down.


The Arc: 🚨 Skyroot becomes India's first space-tech Unicorn as it raises $60 mn at $1.1 bn valuation.

Round led by early Google-backer Sherpalo, along with Singapore's GIC.

The eight-year-old startup was founded by former ISRO scientists Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka.

items.title: In 2023, company believed it could fix low margins and high working capital situations while acquiring an asset from a parent at 800cr. 8-10% EBITDA s...
items.description_html:
In 2023, company believed it could fix low margins and high working capital situations while acquiring an asset from a parent at 800cr. 8-10% EBITDA significantly diluted companies margins.

Company now moving away from the lower-margin, capital-intensive infrastructure and public-sector projects. Come 2026, some engineering intelligence is more important now.

Regardless of the change in the vision & the strategy, the parent turned out wiser to sell the unit at 800cr.

items.description_html:
CARTRADE delivered a strong FY26 with revenue growing 21.5% YoY and EBITDA margin expanding 950 bps to 33%, demonstrating significant operating leverage as revenue outpaced expense growth by 16 percentage points. PAT grew 67.6% YoY with exceptional cash conversion (1.06x PAT).

Consumer and Remarketing segments performed well, though Classifieds (OLX) growth at 15.5% YoY missed management's expectation of acceleration. The company remains well-positioned with strong balance sheet and market share gains in a low-growth auto industry.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 21.5% YoY to ₹7,793 Cr, outpacing low single-digit auto industry growth

EBITDA margin expanded 950 bps to 33.0% with full-year incremental margin at 77%

PAT grew 67.6% YoY (71.4% normalized) to ₹2,435 Cr with EPS of ₹46.80 (+64.3%)

Operating cash flow at 1.06x PAT with FCF at 89.6% of EBITDA

Consumer segment revenue up 29.7% YoY with margins at 41.7%

Remarketing segment revenue up 22.1% YoY with margins at 30.8%

Risk Factors

Classifieds (OLX) segment growth decelerated to 15.5% YoY in Q4, missing management's expected
acceleration beyond 18.5%

Business remains 100% concentrated in auto-linked segments, creating structural industry concentration risk

Cash balance decreased 46% YoY though offset by increase in investments

items.title: RT Anish Moonka: Look down at your big toe. If it used to have hair on it and now it doesn't, blood is probably struggling to reach down that far. Doc...
items.description_html:
RT Anish Moonka
Look down at your big toe. If it used to have hair on it and now it doesn't, blood is probably struggling to reach down that far. Doctors have used this as an early warning sign for decades, and the test that confirms it takes five minutes.

Hair grows because tiny blood vessels feed each follicle with oxygen and nutrients. When fatty deposits clog the arteries higher up your leg, blood barely makes it to your toes. The follicles starve and shut down. Hair falls out and stops coming back.

This is called peripheral artery disease, or PAD, and roughly 200 million people worldwide have it. Mayo Clinic, Johns Hopkins, and the UK's NHS all list bare feet and lower legs as a recognized warning sign. More than half of them feel completely normal in their legs. The hair on their toes is often the only thing giving the disease away.

Hair loss alone doesn't mean you have PAD. Sock friction, age, and hormones can thin foot hair on their own. Doctors look at the bigger picture: shiny tight skin on the calves, cold feet that won't warm up, cuts that take forever to heal, a weak pulse behind your ankle bone, slow-growing toenails. If a few of those signs are happening at once, the hair loss starts to mean something.

The same fatty buildup clogging your leg arteries usually clogs the ones feeding your heart and brain. People with PAD are roughly twice as likely to die of a heart attack or stroke over the next ten years as people without it. Even when they have zero leg symptoms. One long study followed silent PAD patients for a decade and found that only 3 out of every 5 were still alive at the end.

The test is called the Ankle-Brachial Index. A nurse takes your blood pressure at your ankle, then at your arm, and divides one by the other. If the ankle reading is much lower, blood is struggling to reach your feet. The cutoff doctors use is 0.9. Five minutes, doesn't hurt, costs almost nothing.

What turns this from "probably nothing" into "go get checked" is the usual cardiovascular crowd. Smoking, diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, over 50. If a few of those describe you and your toes have gone smooth, ask your doctor for the ABI on your next visit. If they look puzzled, say "ankle blood pressure compared to arm blood pressure." They'll know.

Your toes give away clogged arteries before anything else for one reason. They're at the end of the line.


Nexo Supremo: Vello en los dedos de los pies: una pequeña pista sobre tu salud metabólica y cardiovascular.

Puede parecer un detalle insignificante, pero clínicamente revela información crucial sobre cómo está funcionando tu cuerpo por dentro.

Si no tienes vello en los pies, esto es lo que

items.description_html:
Paytm has also gained Rs 255 crore in FY26 from the depreciation of the Rupee.

Back in December 2024, it sold the rights to the stake in Japan’s PayPay to Softbank for Rs 2,364 crore ($250 mn now) and deployed that money into USD assets.

The USD/rupee has since moved from 84–85 to 94–95.

Since then, PayPay has also been listed in the US and now trades at a $14 bn market cap - Paytm’s stake would have been worth around $700 mn.

However, at that time, amid falling revenues in the backdrop of RBI action, Paytm likely needed as much cash buffer as possible.

items.title: Our cooking maid (didi) has been taking many leaves in recent months. My early "impulse" thoughts were about replacing her if this continues, though w...
items.description_html:
Our cooking maid (didi) has been taking many leaves in recent months. My early "impulse" thoughts were about replacing her if this continues, though we wouldn't want to. I mean she cooks and cares with love and warmth and good taste is a bonus.

So I engaged her in a conversation today to understand more:

"Didi ghar pe saas aur beti ka tabiyat (jaundice) better hua?"

"Haan bhaiya..abhi thoda theek hua hai.."

"Kyu chal raha hai tabet kharab?"

"Bhaiya humare yaha ka pani kharab aata hai..recently to aur kharab ho gaya tha. Peene k paani mai kitanu rehta hai.."

On probing more, learnt the water condition was really a problem (many others fell sick too) and is now better after raising complains.

On probing more I learn that they do not use a water filter but instead a cloth (jaali) on BMC water for drinking purpose. Not sure if this an awareness problem or a situational hazard (she is the main bread winner of house afaik).

Now placing an order for a basic water filter to give/ gift her. We would be spending as much on a dinner outing (in her absence) as the cost of a basic water purifier. It makes economic sense on my wallet, and maybe for my conscience too.

items.title: Something is off in the labor market: In February, US employers cut -448,000 jobs, the largest monthly decline since July 2020. Then, in March, US emp...
items.description_html:
Something is off in the labor market:

In February, US employers cut -448,000 jobs, the largest monthly decline since July 2020.

Then, in March, US employers increased hiring by +655,000 MoM, the largest monthly increase on record, excluding the 2020 pandemic period.

As a result, total hiring across the US economy rose to 5.55 million in March, the highest since February 2024.

This pushed the hiring rate up +0.4 percentage points, to 3.5%, the highest since May 2024, but still below the 2017-2019 pre-pandemic average of 3.8%

This is all despite massive layoff announcements occurring in the first 4 months of the year.

Labor market data is showing conflicting signs.

items.title: RT Chandra R. Srikanth: 🚨🚨 Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace becomes India's first space-tech unicorn; Raises $60 million from Ram Shriram's She...
items.description_html:
RT Chandra R. Srikanth
🚨🚨 Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace becomes India's first space-tech unicorn; Raises $60 million from Ram Shriram's Sherpalo, GIC and BlackRock

Influential tech investor Ram Shriram, among the first backers of Google will be joining Skyroot's board

Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace, a private space launch company, has raised $60 million from Ram Shriram's Sherpalo, Singapore's sovereign wealth fund GIC, BlackRock, the founders of renewable energy company Greenko Group, Arkam Ventures, Playbook Partners, Shanghvi Family Office, and other investors, it announced on May 7.

While the founders of the Greenko Group, Playbook Partners and Shanghvi Family Office are all new investors in the company, Sherpalo, GIC, BlackRock and Arkam Ventures are all existing backers of Skyroot Aerospace. The round was co-led by Sherpalo and GIC.

items.title: RT Ananth Rupanagudi: 1880s, View of Eden Gardens and the Burmese Pagoda, Calcutta (Kolkata). Eden Gardens, established in 1840 in Kolkata and named a...
items.description_html:
RT Ananth Rupanagudi
1880s, View of Eden Gardens and the Burmese Pagoda, Calcutta (Kolkata). Eden Gardens, established in 1840 in Kolkata and named after Auckland’s sisters Emily and Fanny Eden, is a historic 50-acre park known for its cricket stadium, botanical garden, and a 19th-century Burmese Pagoda.

The wooden, gold-and-red pagoda was built in 1852 in Prome (Myanmar) and brought to Kolkata by Lord Dalhousie in 1854 as a war trophy.

Originally known as the Auckland Circus Gardens, the park was designed in 1840 by Emily and Fanny Eden, sisters of the then Governor-General Lord Auckland. It was renamed Eden Gardens, influenced both by the Eden sisters and the biblical concept of a paradise on earth. The area was developed from a swampy area into a premier recreational spot featuring a cricket stadium (established later), lakes, and diverse flora.

The pagoda (a Tazaung) was built in 1852 in Prome (modern-day Pyay), Myanmar, by Ma Kin, the widow of the Governor of Prome. Lord Dalhousie, Governor-General of India, saw the pagoda during the Second Anglo-Burmese War and ordered it transported to Calcutta as a symbol of British conquest.

Shipped in 1854 via the ship HMS Sawaraganga and initially stored at Fort William, the pagoda was inaugurated in Eden Gardens in 1856. The structure features distinctive Burmese, tiered, red-and-gold wooden craftsmanship.

The pagoda is located on the northern side of the gardens near a lake. While it remains a significant tourist site, reports indicate it has required restoration to manage wear and tear, and sometimes holds restricted access. #Kolkata #gardens #History @KanchanGupta

items.title: BREAKING: The median sales price for a new single-family home fell -$21,600 MoM, or -5.3% in March, to $387,400, the lowest since July 2021. This mark...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The median sales price for a new single-family home fell -$21,600 MoM, or -5.3% in March, to $387,400, the lowest since July 2021.

This marks the biggest monthly decline since November 2024.

After adjusting for inflation, the median real home price fell -6.3% MoM, to the lowest since 2014.

Meanwhile, the average sale price dropped -3.4% MoM in March, to $503,100, the lowest since mid-2025.

As a result, the gap between the median and the average price is up to $115,700, the widest on record.

This suggests a handful of luxury home sales are inflating the average price upward while most Americans are purchasing at significantly lower price points.

The housing market is weakening beneath the surface.

items.title: Commodity inflation is surging: The Bloomberg Commodity Index is up to 141 points, the highest since February 2013. This index tracks 25 exchange-trad...
items.description_html:
Commodity inflation is surging:

The Bloomberg Commodity Index is up to 141 points, the highest since February 2013.

This index tracks 25 exchange-traded futures contracts across energy, metals, and agriculture.

Energy accounts for the largest weighting, at 39%, followed by agriculture, at 27%, precious metals, at 16%, and industrial metals, at 13%.

This index is now up +28% year-to-date and it has officially surpassed the 2022 energy crisis peak of 140 points.

As a result, commodity prices are now on track for their first annual increase in 4 years.

Inflation is back.

items.title: The physical North Sea oil market has weakened further, with Dated Brent down to $104 (down ~10% in one day). More importantly, physical differentials...
items.description_html:
The physical North Sea oil market has weakened further, with Dated Brent down to $104 (down ~10% in one day). More importantly, physical differentials have narrowed further, with premia at $0-$2 a barrel range, lowest since the early days of the war. The message? Plenty of oil.


Javier Blas: Counterintuitive perhaps, but the physical oil market appears to be calming. Dated Brent is hovering at ~$115 a barrel, and physical differentials have narrowed massively, with grades offered at ~$3 a barrel premium to the benchmark vs the ~$30 a barrel premium of mid-April.

items.description_html: How incumbents are solving the imference-memory problem is so interesting. Model designs & architectures are evolving so fast & likes of NVDA are already investing heavily. The memory issue is mainly because of the transformers & attention problem. I read a very interesting study on state space models too. As model architecture slowly starts to mimic human brain, the inference costs will collapse to zero and no memory issues either. As an investor, you should follow this space closely to know when you call it out.

Saudi Arabia's deficit has more than TRIPLED since Q2 2025.

This comes as oil revenues fell -3% YoY, while government expenditure surged +20% YoY, to ~$103 billion.

However, the full financial impact of the Iran war has not been reflected yet and is expected to appear more clearly in Q2 data.

Saudi Arabia is facing economic pressure amid the Iran War.

items.title: RT Intrinsic Compounding: Every earnings season, thousands of investors run the same stock screeners: PE < 20 ✅ ROE > 15 ✅ Growth > 20% ✅ Then they...
items.description_html:
RT Intrinsic Compounding
Every earnings season, thousands of investors run the same stock screeners:
PE < 20 ✅
ROE > 15 ✅
Growth > 20% ✅
Then they think they’ve found a great stock idea.
What they’ve actually found is a list.

In 1972, Warren Buffett bought See’s Candies — a business no screener would have highlighted.
The numbers looked ordinary, but what Buffett saw was pricing power, customer behaviour, and emotional moat.

That’s the difference between filtering for numbers and filtering for businesses.

This Sunday, we’ll show you how to build a framework to identify truly exceptional businesses — Indian & Global.

✶ Topic: How to Filter Epic Stock Ideas? Indian & International Businesses
✶ Date & Time: 10 May, 1 p.m. (IST)

Use a browser to enrol, mobile apps charge 30% additional fees
✶ Venue: Zoom
☞ Enrolment valid for 365 days.
☞ Recording available 48-72 hours after the live webinar.

What we will cover:
➥ Why are most idea generation processes broken — and what to replace them with?
➥ The industry-first lens: how to identify sectors where the next leg of growth is already visible?
➥ What global businesses teach Indian investors.
➥ SOIC’s framework for spotting high-conviction ideas.
➥ Live breakdowns of Indian & international companies.

This isn’t a tips session.
It’s about building the muscle to generate your own ideas — consistently and intelligently.

items.title: FMCG companies are reporting a much better set in Q4FY26 The key change is that growth is now becoming more volume led After multiple weak quarters of...
items.description_html:
FMCG companies are reporting a much better set in Q4FY26

The key change is that growth is now becoming more volume led

After multiple weak quarters of rural slowdown, inflation pressure & muted discretionary demand, volume growth is coming back across large FMCG names (Nestle, Marico, VBL, HUL, Bajaj Cons, Godrej CP etc)

HUL reported 6% underlying volume growth, which was its strongest volume growth in nearly 3 years.

Nestle delivered one of the strongest sets in the FMCG pack.

Domestic REV growth was around 23% YoY, supported by strong volume growth.

Growth was supported by strong demand recovery, capacity expansion in Maggi & KitKat, rural distribution expansion and higher media spends.

Marico REV grew 22% YoY, while domestic volume growth stood at 9%.

For FY26, India business volume growth was 8%, highest in 7 years.

International business also remained strong with 19% constant currency growth in Q4 & 20% growth in FY26.

VBL reported a strong set with volume led growth.

Cons. REV grew 18%, driven by 16% volume growth & 2% realization growth.

India volume growth stood at 14%, while international volumes grew 21%.

Godrej Consumer Products : India business delivered 10% sales growth with 8% volume growth.

Growth was driven by home care, household insecticides, air fresheners & fabric care.

The key change is that India business volume growth is improving and management commentary remains positive for FY27.

Bajaj Consumer Care : REV grew 31% YoY
PAT more than doubled YoY to 64 Cr

If rural recovery sustains & input cost inflation remains manageable, FMCG can see a much better next few quarters.

Although one caveat valuations are not cheap, since the sector always commanded premium valuations

We recently initiated coverage on a business sitting at an inflection point.

The setup is linked to improving FMCG demand trends, where large brands continue to outsource more manufacturing to scalable & reliable partners.

After a heavy capex cycle, fixed assets have almost doubled, As utilization ramps up, operating leverage should start reflecting meaningfully, with PAT expected to nearly double over the next 2 years.
(Connect to know more about our research services.)

items.description_html:
BREAKING: Anthropic's pre-IPO valuation surges to a record $1.2 trillion, rising another +20% in 7 days.

This officially puts Anthropic's implied valuation up +900% since October 2025, per onchain pre-IPO trading data.

Pre-IPO instruments trading onchain on Jupiter, backed 1:1 by SPV exposure, are providing a real-time proxy for the company’s implied IPO valuation.

This now makes Anthropic ~20% larger than OpenAI's pre-IPO implied valuation.

If Anthropic were to IPO at a $1.2 trillion valuation, it would be the 11th most valuable public company in the world.

The AI Revolution is accelerating.

items.title: Bajaj Auto board has approved a buyback of up to 46.94 Lakh equity shares Buyback size - 5,633 Cr Buyback price - 12,000/share % of paid-up equity - 1...
items.description_html:
Bajaj Auto board has approved a buyback of up to 46.94 Lakh equity shares

Buyback size - 5,633 Cr
Buyback price - 12,000/share
% of paid-up equity - 1.68%
Route - Tender offer

Buybacks are again becoming an important trend

The Corporate Laws Amendment Bill 2026 proposes allowing certain prescribed companies to undertake up to 2 buybacks in a financial year, with a minimum gap of 6 months between them.

SEBI has also floated a consultation paper to re-introduce open market buybacks through the stock exchange route.

On the taxation side, buyback taxation had shifted to shareholders from Oct'24, where buyback proceeds were treated as deemed dividend.

Now, Budget 2026 has proposed taxing buyback proceeds as capital gains instead of deemed dividend, which makes buybacks more attractive.

With more regulatory flexibility & better tax treatment, more cash rich companies may start using buybacks as a preferred capital allocation tool.

Post the proposed buyback rule change, these companies have announced or are going through buybacks:

Bajaj Auto, Aurobindo Pharma, Windlas Biotech, EMA Partners, Jagsonpal Pharma, Rolex Rings, Cyient, Kajaria Ceramics, Garware Technical Fibres, Prime Securities, Wipro.

items.title: GODREJCP delivered mixed Q4 FY26 results - revenue growth of 11% met guidance but EBITDA declined 0.6% vs double-digit growth target. Normalized PAT g...
items.description_html:
GODREJCP delivered mixed Q4 FY26 results - revenue growth of 11% met guidance but EBITDA declined 0.6% vs double-digit growth target. Normalized PAT grew 19.5% YoY excluding exceptional items. India segment showed strong margin expansion (+114 bps) while Africa profitability compressed despite robust revenue growth.

Cash generation remained strong with FCF of ₹1,919 crore. The significant EBITDA guidance miss and margin compression in Africa are key concerns, though the Latin America turnaround and Muuchstac acquisition provide future growth drivers.

Positive Factors

Normalized PAT grew 19.5% YoY to ₹516.24 crore in Q4, adjusting for exceptional items

India segment EBIT grew 14.6% YoY with 114 bps margin expansion to 24.5%

Operating cash flow strong at ₹2,488.46 crore with 1.34x CFO/PAT ratio

Free cash flow generation strong at ₹1,918.94 crore

Others segment (Latin America) revenue grew 25.6% YoY with 40.2% EBIT growth

FY26 standalone EBITDA margin at 25.7% within 24-26% guidance range

Consolidated revenue grew 8.4% YoY for FY26, meeting high single-digit guidance

Risk Factors

FY26 consolidated EBITDA declined 0.6% YoY vs double-digit growth guidance - significant miss

Africa segment EBIT declined 3.2% YoY with 293 bps margin compression despite 20.4% revenue growth

EBITDA margin contracted 152 bps YoY to 19.2% in Q4

Current ratio declined to 0.91x from 1.06x YoY, indicating liquidity pressure

Raw material cost as % of sales improved but employee expenses up 23.9% YoY outpacing revenue

items.title: BREAKING: According to our analysis, ~$920 million worth of crude oil shorts were taken 70 minutes before an Axios report claimed the US and Iran were...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: According to our analysis, ~$920 million worth of crude oil shorts were taken 70 minutes before an Axios report claimed the US and Iran were near a "14-point" deal to end the war.

At 3:40 AM ET today, nearly 10,000 contracts worth of crude oil shorts were taken without any major news.

This is equivalent to ~$920 million in notional value, an unusually large trade for 3:40 AM ET.

At 4:50 AM ET, just 70 minutes later, Axios reported that the US is "close" to a "memorandum of understanding" to end the Iran War.

By 7:00 AM ET, oil prices had fallen over -12% with these crude oil shorts gaining approximately +$125 million.

Minutes later, Iran launched the "Persian Gulf Strait Authority" and oil prices surged +8%.

What just happened?

items.title: VIMTALABS delivered solid FY26 results with 18.4% revenue growth meeting its guidance range, and strong cash generation with 1.90x CFO/PAT conversion....
items.description_html:
VIMTALABS delivered solid FY26 results with 18.4% revenue growth meeting its guidance range, and strong cash generation with 1.90x CFO/PAT conversion. The company significantly deleveraged with 60.9% debt reduction achieving ₹637 million net cash position.

Q4 showed margin inflection with 152bps YoY EBITDA margin expansion. Key catalyst is the Biologics services launch targeted for Q1 FY27, which could accelerate growth toward management's aspiration to double the business in 3 years.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 18.4% YoY to ₹4,072.90 million, meeting management's 15-20% growth guidance

PAT grew 15.25% YoY to ₹775.12 million with strong recovery in Q4

Operating cash flow surged 56.4% YoY to ₹1,471.42 million with 1.90x CFO/PAT ratio

Free cash flow positive at ₹473.42 million despite ₹998 million capex

Total borrowings reduced 60.9% YoY to ₹33.32 million, net cash position strengthened to ₹637.29 million

Trade receivables flat YoY despite 18.4% revenue growth showing improved collection efficiency

Q4 EBITDA margin expanded 152 bps YoY to 37.61%

Risk Factors

Total expenses grew 19.9% YoY, outpacing revenue growth of 18.4%

Other expenses grew 31.7% YoY, contributing to margin pressure

EBITDA margin compressed 47 bps YoY for full year from 36.24% to 35.77%

Revenue run rate of ₹500 crore target was missed at ₹407.29 crore

Q4 quarterly revenue target of ₹120-125 crore was missed at ₹109.25 crore

items.title: BMW delivered a strong Q4 recovery with 33.3% YoY revenue growth and 88.1% PAT growth, but FY26 results significantly missed management's prior aggres...
items.description_html:
BMW delivered a strong Q4 recovery with 33.3% YoY revenue growth and 88.1% PAT growth, but FY26 results significantly missed management's prior aggressive guidance across all key metrics. The company is in a heavy capex phase with CWIP up 256% and debt more than doubling to fund the Bokaro project, expected to start generating revenue from Q1 FY27.

While operating leverage was evident in Q4 with EBITDA margin expansion to 27.5%, rising material costs (69.3% YoY increase) and finance costs (31.8% YoY increase) remain key concerns.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue grew 33.3% YoY to ₹20,949.56 Lakh with strong sequential recovery

Q4 PAT grew 88.1% YoY to ₹3,315.66 Lakh demonstrating operating leverage

Q4 EBITDA margin expanded to 27.5% from 22.2% in Q4 FY25 (527 bps YoY expansion)

FY26 EBITDA margin expanded 125 bps to 24.8% despite material cost pressures

Other expenses declined 16.2% YoY to ₹18,902 Lakh, partially offsetting cost pressures

Operating cash flow to PAT ratio healthy at 1.02x

Risk Factors

FY26 revenue of ₹66,523 Lakh significantly missed management's guidance of ₹109,909 Lakh (39.5%
shortfall)

FY26 PAT of ₹8,112 Lakh missed management's guidance of ₹10,507 Lakh (22.8% shortfall)

Cost of materials consumed jumped 69.3% YoY in Q4, far outpacing revenue growth

Finance costs rose 31.8% YoY to ₹1,888 Lakh due to debt funding Bokaro project

Net Debt/Equity ratio deteriorated to 0.41x from 0.16x, reflecting leverage increase

Operating cash flow declined 33.5% YoY to ₹8,317 Lakh due to working capital pressure

BREAKING: Iran has launched a new website called the "Persian Gulf Strait Authority" to oversee traffic through the Strait of Hormuz just minutes after an Axios report claimed a deal was near to end the war and reopen Hormuz.

items.title: FAIRCHEMOR reported a significant guidance miss in FY26 with revenue of ₹459.65 Cr falling 54% below the ₹1,000 Cr target and EBITDA margin at 4.70%...
items.description_html:
FAIRCHEMOR reported a significant guidance miss in FY26 with revenue of ₹459.65 Cr falling 54% below the ₹1,000 Cr target and EBITDA margin at 4.70% versus 23% guidance. However, Q4 showed sequential recovery with revenue up 16.77% QoQ and PAT turning positive at ₹36.92 Cr from a loss.

Cash generation was exceptionally strong with CFO/PAT at 7.5x, and working capital improved substantially. Management has withdrawn guidance and now targets 8-10% EBITDA margin for FY27, representing a 70% EBITDA growth requirement from current levels - execution risk remains high.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue recovered 16.77% QoQ to ₹116.93 Cr from ₹100.13 Cr in Q3

Q4 PAT turned positive at ₹36.92 Cr vs loss of ₹0.97 Cr in Q3

Q4 EBITDA margin improved to 6.87% vs 4.17% in Q3 (270 bps sequential improvement)

Operating cash flow strong at ₹416 Cr vs PAT of ₹55.41 Cr (7.5x conversion)

Trade receivables reduced by ₹180.66 Cr YoY improving working capital

Share buyback of ₹35.14 Cr completed returning capital to shareholders

Risk Factors

FY26 revenue declined 14.55% YoY to ₹459.65 Cr vs guidance of ₹1,000 Cr (missed by 54%)

FY26 EBITDA margin collapsed to 4.70% vs guidance of 23% (missed by 1,830 bps)

FY26 PAT declined 74.79% YoY to ₹55.41 Cr from ₹219.74 Cr

FY26 EBITDA margin compressed 325 bps YoY from 7.95% to 4.70%

Working capital: inventories rose 4.47% YoY despite revenue decline

Debt-to-equity ratio increased to 0.32x from 0.21x YoY

items.title: Gargi Jewellers delivered strong FY26 results with revenue growth of 48.36% on adjusted base, significantly exceeding the 35% guidance. Store expansio...
items.description_html:
Gargi Jewellers delivered strong FY26 results with revenue growth of 48.36% on adjusted base, significantly exceeding the 35% guidance. Store expansion was aggressive with 32 new stores added versus a target of 10-15.

However, margins compressed 132 bps YoY and cash conversion remained weak at 35% of PAT due to inventory build-up for new stores. The company remains on track to meet its FY27 targets of 20 new stores and 35% revenue growth.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 48.36% YoY on adjusted base, exceeding 35% guidance

32 new stores added vs target of 10-15 stores
PAT grew 8.76% YoY to ₹31.33 Cr

Total store count increased from 94 to 126

Company remains debt-free, funding growth through equity and internal accruals

Other income grew 154.35% YoY to ₹534.27 Lakhs

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin contracted 132 bps YoY to 29.99%

Operating cash flow was weak at 35% of PAT due to inventory build-up

Inventory days increased from 92 to 118

PAT margin contracted from 22.80% to 20.97%

ROE declined from 28.90% to 22.08%

Cash and cash equivalents fell 95.69%

#Flash | In a key decision, the Union Cabinet led by Narendra Modi approved a proposal to grant the National Song Vande Mataram the same status as the National Anthem Jana Gana Mana. The move includes a proposed amendment to the Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act, bringing ‘Vande Mataram’ under the same legal protections and penalties as the anthem.
The decision comes after a major electoral victory in West Bengal and coincides with the song’s 150th anniversary.

items.title: South Indian Bank delivered mixed Q4 results with dramatic asset quality improvement (GNPA down 177bps to 1.43%) and strong capital position (CAR 19.6...
items.description_html:
South Indian Bank delivered mixed Q4 results with dramatic asset quality improvement (GNPA down 177bps to 1.43%) and strong capital position (CAR 19.66%), but missed NIM guidance as net interest income declined 1.4% FY-on-FY.

The strategic pivot to retail banking is working with segment profit up 130% YoY, while corporate banking remains under pressure. Normalized PAT growth of 9.7% (excluding one-time provision reversal) better reflects core performance than the headline 19% beat.

Positive Factors

Gross NPA ratio improved 177 bps YoY to 1.43%, Net NPA improved 63 bps to 0.29%

Capital Adequacy Ratio at 19.66% well above regulatory minimum

Retail banking profit surged 130% YoY to 38,417 Lakhs

Loan growth at 15.85% YoY exceeded 12% guidance

Cost-to-income ratio improved to 56.43% from 57.16%
CFO to PAT ratio strong at 2.68x

Dividend increased to ₹0.45 from ₹0.40 per share

Risk Factors

NIM missed guidance of exceed 3% - full year NII declined 1.4% YoY

Corporate banking profit fell 36% YoY to 8,078 Lakhs

Digital banking loss widened to (10,376) Lakhs

PPOP declined 14.9% YoY in Q4 indicating core operational weakness

One-time provision reversal of 4,310 Lakhs inflated reported PAT

items.title: RML delivered strong earnings growth with PAT up 466% YoY in Q4 and 185% for FY26, driven by revenue growth of 16% YoY and operating leverage. EBITDA ...
items.description_html:
RML delivered strong earnings growth with PAT up 466% YoY in Q4 and 185% for FY26, driven by revenue growth of 16% YoY and operating leverage. EBITDA margin expanded 46 bps to 9.20%, though this remains below the double-digit target.

The company achieved significant debt reduction of ₹73 Cr and positive FCF of ₹84 Cr, while international sales surged 27% YoY demonstrating competitive strength. However, margin expansion and debt reduction targets were missed by about half, suggesting execution is good but not meeting full commitments.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 16.26% YoY to ₹1,047.87 Cr in Q4 and 13.42% to ₹3,862.92 Cr for FY26

PAT grew 466.87% YoY to ₹36.96 Cr in Q4 and 185.47% to ₹107.48 Cr for FY26

EBITDA margin expanded 30 bps YoY to 9.48% in Q4 and 46 bps to 9.20% for FY26

Finance costs declined 32.5% YoY to ₹12.49 Cr in Q4

Net debt reduced by ₹73.41 Cr to ₹704.77 Cr

International sales grew 27% YoY, significantly outpacing domestic channels

Free cash flow positive at ₹84.37 Cr for FY26

Net worth increased 11.9% to ₹750.56 Cr

Risk Factors

EBITDA margin at 9.20% remains below double-digit target of 10%

Margin expansion of 46 bps missed the 100 bps synergy target

Debt reduction of ₹73.41 Cr achieved vs target of ₹150 Cr

Cost of materials consumed grew 21.3% YoY, faster than revenue, pressuring gross margins

New annualized order wins of ₹712 Cr lower than ₹800 Cr mentioned for Q1 FY26

items.title: CGPOWER delivered strong Q4 FY26 with 25% revenue growth and 32.5% PAT growth, driven by exceptional 50% YoY growth in Power Systems segment where mar...
items.description_html:
CGPOWER delivered strong Q4 FY26 with 25% revenue growth and 32.5% PAT growth, driven by exceptional 50% YoY growth in Power Systems segment where margins expanded 287 bps to 23.8%. The balance sheet was transformed via ₹3000 cr QIP, leaving the company near debt-free with record ₹14859 cr order book.

Industrial Systems segment remains a drag with 16.5% PBIT decline, while new semiconductor business is ramping with ₹155.6 cr revenue but losses of ₹37.4 cr. Execution quality is superior with capacity expansion ahead of schedule.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 25.0% YoY to ₹3441.76 crores in Q4

PAT grew 32.5% YoY to ₹363.46 crores, EPS at ₹2.32 up 30.3%

EBITDA margin expanded 95 bps YoY to 13.55%

Power Systems segment revenue grew 49.7% YoY with PBIT up 60.6%

Power Systems PBIT margin expanded 287 bps to 23.8%

Semiconductors segment started contributing with ₹155.60 crores revenue

Total equity doubled to ₹8198.25 crores post ₹3000 cr QIP

Order book at record ₹14859 crores for standalone company

Company near debt-free with borrowings at only ₹0.26 crores

Government grants of ₹668.19 crores received for semiconductor business

Risk Factors

Industrial Systems segment PBIT declined 16.5% YoY with margin compression of 202 bps to 8.95%

Semiconductor segment PBIT loss of ₹37.42 crores in Q4, full year loss at ₹107.86 crores

Employee costs up 53.6% YoY due to hiring for new semiconductor business

Working capital increased significantly - trade receivables up 45.5% YoY, inventories up 39.3% YoY

Operating cash flow declined 23.3% YoY to ₹702.45 crores despite higher profits

Railway business issues in Industrial Systems not yet fully resolved

items.description_html:
RADICO delivered a major guidance beat in FY26 with EBITDA margin expansion of 299 bps (vs 150 bps target) and net debt reduction of 57.4% (vs 35-40% target). PAT grew 74.9% YoY to ₹604.5 Crore with operating leverage visible as net revenue outpaced expense growth.

The balance sheet transformation is complete with debt-to-equity at 0.10x, positioning the company for sustained premiumization-driven growth.

Positive Factors

EBITDA margin expanded 299 bps YoY to 16.88%, beating 150 bps guidance target

Net Debt reduced 57.4% YoY to ₹244 Crore, exceeding 35-40% reduction target

PAT grew 74.9% YoY to ₹604.5 Crore with 94.9% growth in Q4

Revenue grew 22.7% YoY to ₹20,976.4 Crore

Operating cash flow increased 105% YoY to ₹742.6 Crore

Debt-to-Equity ratio improved from 0.23x to 0.10x

Finance costs declined 13.2% YoY due to deleveraging

Risk Factors

Two exceptional charges of ₹16.55 Crore for municipal tax and labor code changes

Selling & distribution expenses grew 34.6% YoY, faster than revenue

Slight QoQ revenue decline of 4.45% in Q4

Q4 PAT at ₹179.5 Crore is highest in 5 quarters but shows sequential slowdown

items.title: I think this is genuinely strange as a question to ask. Gamestop is buying EBay for $55bn - but it's a merger with part stock part cash payout. This i...
items.description_html:
I think this is genuinely strange as a question to ask. Gamestop is buying EBay for $55bn - but it's a merger with part stock part cash payout. This is a very common occurance and it's strange the anchors were using some weird math of counting current market cap of Gamestop as if it would be given as part of the deal.

Gamestock issues about $28bn of fresh stock. It has $28bn of cash plus debt. A shareholder of ebay gets Gamestock shares plus cash, equivalent to the amount per share that Gamestop is buying ebay for.

Gamestop's current market cap is $11bn. It will therefore become - if you want to "math" it - $11bn+$28bn fresh issue = $38bn. And for that, what matters is the effective diluted EPS of the merged entity, which is in the GME presentation.

I know he's gruff. But he's right in saying he doesn't understand the question. It's like a trick question.


Mangalam Maloo: I feel you, @andrewrsorkin! I do!

This is the funniest interview I’ve seen in my life.

There have been times, we’ve asked tough questions and managements have been evasive, but none as blatantly nonchalant.

items.title: RT ANI: #WATCH | Mumbai | Member of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister, Sanjeev Sanyal says, “The relative performance of the Indian...
items.description_html:
RT ANI
#WATCH | Mumbai | Member of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister, Sanjeev Sanyal says, “The relative performance of the Indian states shows one or two very clear trends. The first is the much talk about the North-South divide, which is not the real economic crisis in the country… It is actually an East-West divide where the eastern half of India is distinctively poorer than the western half of the country… The key to the Eastern half of India is reviving Kolkata, specifically because the high growth all over the country is ultimately driven by a handful of high-growth urban hubs… In that context, eastern India needs a high growth hub… Kolkata already has some clusters and a long, glorious history. Despite the last half century of decline, I would argue that the secret to reviving the eastern half of India must be about the revival of Kolkata, specifically.”

items.description_html:
Coforge - Strong set🔥

Q4FY26
• REV 🔼30% - 4,450 Cr
• EBITDA 🔼56% - 917 Cr
• EBIT 🔼76% - 737 Cr

FY26 was a standout year for Coforge, with 29% YoY growth in USD terms & EBIT margin expansion of 370 bps

FY26
• REV 🔼36% - 16,421 Cr
• EBITDA 🔼77% - 3,046 Cr
• EBIT 🔼83% - 2,365 Cr
• PAT 🔼92% - 1,556 Cr

Solid Cash Generation🔥
CFO : $203 Mn vs 146 Mn
FCF : $135 Mn vs 80 Mn

Order intake remained strong.
• Q4FY26 order intake - $648 Mn
• FY26 total order intake - $2.26 Bn
• 12 Month executable order book - $1.75 Bn
• Executable order book 🔼16% YoY

Company believes global enterprise AI spends can grow from $160-180 Bn to $750-800 Bn over the next 5 years. Agentic AI spends are expected to grow from $8-10 Bn to $120-140 Bn over the same period.
Positioning itself as an AI native engineering services company.

Management highlighted that AI is creating new value pools for IT services rather than only being a disruption risk

2026 was a breakout year for the company

FY27 outlook remains strong
Management expects robust REV growth in FY27 & has guided for consolidated EBITDA margin of more than 20.5%.

With $1.75 Bn executable order book, 20.5%+ EBITDA margin guidance, Coforge continues to outperform the sector

items.title: SJS Enterprises - Solid Set, Consistency continues Q4FY26 • REV 🔼30% - 260 Cr • EBITDA 🔼53% - 81 Cr • PAT 🔼45% - 49 Cr 🔥 FY26 • REV �...
items.description_html:
SJS Enterprises - Solid Set, Consistency continues

Q4FY26
• REV 🔼30% - 260 Cr
• EBITDA 🔼53% - 81 Cr
• PAT 🔼45% - 49 Cr 🔥

FY26
• REV 🔼26% - 955 Cr
• EBITDA 🔼42% - 288 Cr
• PAT 🔼45% - 172 Cr 🔥

Solid Return Ratio & Cash Generation
ROCE - 36% ,ROE - 20% 🔥
CFO - 224 Cr , FCFF - 143 Cr
Net Cash on b/s- 244 Cr

Operating leverage continues to play out

The key highlight was continued Sector outperformance

SJS delivered 41% YoY growth in automotive business versus 19% YoY growth for the underlying Industry in Q4FY26

For FY26, delivered 35% growth in the 2W + PV segment versus 11% industry growth, meaning the company grew more than 3x the industry

Growth was led by strong traction in both 2W & PV segments.

Q4FY26 segment growth
• 2W segment 🔼41%
• PV segment 🔼41%
• Domestic business 🔼26%
• Exports 🔼75% - 26 Cr

Exports formed around 10% of Q4FY26 REV and continue to scale well

Margin expansion was driven by better product mix, higher export contribution & operational efficiency

Continues to benefit from premiumisation & new age aesthetic products

Company is expanding into higher value products like optical plastics, display systems, illuminated logos, IMD, IML & IME products.

FY27 outlook remains strong
Management expects SJS to continue outperforming the underlying industry growth by 1.5-2x supported by:
• Premiumisation
• Building mega OEM accounts
• Export growth
• New customer additions
• New product pipeline
• Higher kit value per vehicle
• Capacity expansion

Current order book to be executed in FY27 is already over 85% of FY27 forecasted REV

items.description_html:
Interesting - Every 2nd person who bought a Maruti Suzuki India car was a first-time car Buyer!

In Q4FY26, First-time buyer was about 51%, replacement buyers were 18%, and additional car buyers were 31%!

H1FY26 - 42% were first-time buyers, Q3FY26 - 48%, and Q4FY26 - 51%.

It is a very clear signal that the government's intended support for the first-time buyer is showing results!

FY26 - Maruti recorded its highest-ever annual sales of 24.2 lakh vehicles, which includes the highest-ever export of 447,000 vehicles.

Happy to see that almost 11-12L families bought their first car :)

items.title: Freshworks CEO Dennis Woodside on why it's laying off over 500 employees: "Over half of our code is originated in AI today....that is definitely chang...
items.description_html:
Freshworks CEO Dennis Woodside on why it's laying off over 500 employees:

"Over half of our code is originated in AI today....that is definitely changing the amount of people we need to build product."

The other reason is restructuring the sales team to focus on EX, or IT service management biz (Freshservice), which is growing at 27% YoY. This will account for 60% of revenues by year's end.

The original CX (Freshdesk) business is now growing at just 6% YoY and will continue in "low single digits."


The Arc: 🚨 Freshworks has decided to lay off 11% of its workforce, or about 500 employees.

This is the second major layoff at the Chennai-based software firm in the last two years - in Nov 2024, it fired 660 employees, or 13% of the workforce.

Freshworks reported a 16% rise in revenues

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: MS on PNB UW, TP Rs 88 Core revenues were 5% below MSe. Core PPOP adjusted for reversal of retirement provisions (due to higher bond y...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
MS on PNB
UW, TP Rs 88
Core revenues were 5% below MSe.
Core PPOP adjusted for reversal of retirement provisions (due to higher bond yields) also missed MSe
Reversal of retirement provisions, lower credit costs (owing to certain lumpy reversals) & bad loan recoveries drove a strong headline PAT beat.

CLSA on PNB
O-P, TP Rs 135
PNB reported an optical PAT beat of 16% driven by lower-than-expected Opex and credit costs, despite a 5% miss on total income
Key disappointment was NII, which declined 3% QoQ despite growth in its loan book.
Loan growth of 13.7% YoY was slightly below our estimate of 15% YoY.
Deposit growth of 9% YoY was in line with last quarter, but clearly a slowdown from FY25
CASA ratio of 36% was steady which is a decent outcome.
Asset quality performance was decent with the gross slippage ratio declining 20bps YoY to 0.9%
Cut FY27-28CL PAT estimates by 3% on account of lower NII

Jefferies on PNB
Buy, TP Rs 130
Q4 profit of Rs52bn (+14% YoY) was ahead of est, as reversal of retirement benefits & write-back of provisions compensated for weaker NII.
Loan growth improved to 14% YoY, but NIM compression dragged NII.
Headroom in LDR allows PNB to grow loans despite soft deposit growth of 9%.
Slippages fell YoY & core credit costs are controlled.
Ability to sustain ROA despite transition to ECL will be key; valuations reasonable at 0.8x PB.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: Transformer industry trends from concalls and PPTs post results 1. Lead times have increased for Bushings and insulators. 2. Transformer oil prices ha...
items.description_html:
Transformer industry trends from concalls and PPTs post results

1. Lead times have increased for Bushings and insulators.

2. Transformer oil prices have increased. Transformer oil is 8-12% of overall costs. Key suppliers are the likes of APAR and Savita oil.

3. US tariffs have come down from 50% to 25% to finally 10%.

4. Middle East exports have impacted sales.

5. Backlogs are bigger for Power transformers vs IDT or distribution transformers at the moment.

Near term margins can be weak due to the reasons above inspite of a healthy order book & enquiry pipeline.

Birlas have run several businesses successfully, however Vodafone Idea is still fragile and a turnaround would take a larger effort.

items.title: RT Shereen Bhan: Top highlights of my conversation with ITC’s Sanjeev Puri 1. Demand not impacted yet, but raw material inflation rising. Lower than ...
items.description_html:
RT Shereen Bhan
Top highlights of my conversation with ITC’s Sanjeev Puri
1. Demand not impacted yet, but raw material inflation rising. Lower than the 8-10% impact that HUL spoke of.
2. Some price hikes have been taken in the FMCG business, more likely.
3. Tax impact on cigarettes business significant, typically see a volume Degrowth when taxes are hiked. Have taken a 50-60 % hike in prices, staggering the rest.
4. 20,000 cr capex over the next 4-5 years
5. Big bet on the agri biz, broken even in year 3
6. Hotel biz seen a hit because of West Asia conflict, starting to improve now. Will add a hotel a month, expect domestic demand to drive growth
7. Working towards our target of being India’s number 1 FMCG company
More below 👇 @CNBCTV18News @CNBCTV18Live #CNBCTV18Market

Lending is like sitting on a three legged stool.

This is such a simple but powerful way to understand lending businesses

Overall, SBFC concalls are always worth reading

items.title: RT menaka doshi: Is the Tamil Nadu Model at risk? For over four decades Tamil Nadu has enjoyed what’s been popularly referred to as “bipartisan cons...
items.description_html:
RT menaka doshi
Is the Tamil Nadu Model at risk?

For over four decades Tamil Nadu has enjoyed what’s been popularly referred to as “bipartisan consensus.”

As power alternated between the two main regional parties, DMK and AIADMK, one thing remained broadly constant — the drive to industrialize.

Now, the election of a newbie chief minister casts a shadow on this implicit arrangement.

Will Vijay build a tri-partisan consensus?

Click here for my latest #IndiaEdition 👇

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/newsletters/2026-05-05/vijay-s-tamil-nadu-win-may-benefit-chandrababu-naidu-s-andhra-pradesh?srnd=undefined

Both PMS and MF have funds that have not beaten the Index and many PMS have infact beaten the Index and All mutual funds over years!

Please understand - Anti PMS narrative is a win for MF mainly because a PMS takes HNI AUM away from MF!

You can't call an MF fund manager and ask him market take, but you surely can ask a PMS fund manager.

Both have their pros and cons, but the main thing is keeping the user on his wealth growth Path.

Have invested in both Mutual funds and PMS, I know the difference between the two and truly appreciate both. Underperformance in MF or PMS or stocks anyway should not be tolerated

items.title: Mahindra & Mahindra – Q4 FY26 summary Strong earnings across Auto, Farm, Mahindra Finance and the growth businesses, with ROE at 20% and management c...
items.description_html:
Mahindra & Mahindra – Q4 FY26 summary

Strong earnings across Auto, Farm, Mahindra Finance and the growth businesses, with ROE at 20% and management calling FY26 a year of continued strong execution.

Business-wise breakdown

Auto: FY26 PAT grew 33% YoY to ₹7,842 crore. SUV volume grew 19% YoY to 1,118k, SUV revenue market share rose to 25.3% (+260 bps YoY), and LCV market share improved to 52.3%. Auto business margin improved to 10.9% in Q4 FY26. 100% PLI compliant

Farm: FY26 PAT grew 13% YoY to ₹4,298 crore. Domestic tractor volumes grew 24% YoY to 526k, market share improved to 43.6% (+30 bps YoY), and farm standalone PBIT margin rose to 19.8% in FY26.

Mahindra Finance: PAT grew 60% YoY to ₹2,855 crore; AUM grew 12% YoY to ₹1,34,000 crore, and GS3 improved to 3.41%. 99% secured portfolio. Udaan-important tech-platform for disbursals.

Tech Mahindra: PAT grew 14% YoY to ₹4,811 crore. TCV was $3,794 million and EBIT margin improved to 12.6% (+290 bps YoY), showing margin-led recovery.

Growth gems: Strong momentum in real estate, logistics, aerostructures, last mile mobility, hospitality and trucks & buses. 7x PAT move in Lifespaces and improving scale in logistics / EV / aero.

Key guidance & management commentary

Management is guiding for mid-single-digit industry growth in Auto & Farm, but expects Mahindra to do better because of product strength, market share gains and capacity additions. 15–18% SUV growth is possible if capacity ramps as planned.

Auto margins are expected to stay healthy, though the mix includes eSUV contract manufacturing which dilutes reported margin. Auto standalone margin was 10.4% for FY26. 10+ new launches in SUV and LCV by FY31

In Farm, the big focus is the new tractor range and product refreshes. (with transmission & power). Exited Sampo, Erkunt business units.

AI is now being treated as a real operating lever, with management citing ₹4,100 crore+ revenue potential in FY27 from AI-led adoption, alongside shorter time-to-market (10% reduced).

The strongest combination is market-share gains + margin expansion + high ROE. Auto is still the key earnings engine, Farm is stable with good margins, Finance is scaling with improving asset quality, and TechM is adding margin-led support.

Auto growth is backed by SUV leadership and EV traction, going to be less supply and more demand. No impact because of fuel prices fluctuations.
Farm is backed by new product cycles,
financial services improving with cleaner asset quality and scale.

items.title: RT Chandra R. Srikanth: 🚨 Exclusive: Cognizant may cut 12,000-15,000 jobs globally; bulk of cuts in India IT services major Cognizant is weighing a...
items.description_html:
RT Chandra R. Srikanth
🚨 Exclusive: Cognizant may cut 12,000-15,000 jobs globally; bulk of cuts in India

IT services major Cognizant is weighing a reduction in workforce that could impact 12,000-15,000 employees worldwide, with the bulk of the layoffs expected in India, multiple sources told Moneycontrol.

On April 29, the Nasdaq-listed Cognizant said it expects to incur between $230 million and $320 million in severance costs under its newly announced Project Leap, without disclosing the number of potential job losses.

The company employs more than 357,000 people, with most, over 250,000, based in India.

The layoffs estimate is based on back of the envelope calculations using typical salary and severance assumptions across geographies.

In India, the average annual salary is assumed to be around Rs 15 lakh.

With severance ranging between four to six months, the cost per employee rises to roughly $50,000, meaning fewer employees can be impacted for the same level of provisioning.

Combining these assumptions across regions yields a global estimate in the range of 12,000 to 15,000 employees.

Sources said these calculations are indicative and based on broad assumptions around salary, tenure, and geographic mix, and the final number could vary depending on how the restructuring is executed.

With @shaw_reshab

https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/information-technology/cognizant-may-cut-12000-15000-jobs-globally-bulk-of-cuts-in-india-13908373.html

items.title: The loudest predictions of Indian IT's structural decline tend to come from people who have not spent any meaningful time inside an enterprise IT orga...
items.description_html:
The loudest predictions of Indian IT's structural decline tend to come from people who have not spent any meaningful time inside an enterprise IT organisation.
Not as a delivery lead.
Not as an account director.
Not as a transformation architect.
Not as the senior person who has had to land a cutover in production over a weekend with three regulators on the call.
Announcement comes, the commentary follows. While it is well-presented, mostly, technically correct. Bit, it is operationally weak.

Select section wants Industry to be impacted. It's mainly because of a position, perception they have about the industry and finally want that to be vindicated on.

The sector is undergoing through a reset, no doubt. If you think the industry has no strength to stand on, you will be proven wrong.

items.title: RT Normal Guy: India reserves sit at $698+ billion but that number hides a lot. Gold is $131 billion of that, you cannot sell gold fast to pay for cru...
items.description_html:
RT Normal Guy
India reserves sit at $698+ billion but that number hides a lot.

Gold is $131 billion of that, you cannot sell gold fast to pay for crude imports. RBI forward book is now $104 billion in net short dollar positions, a record.

Strip both out and usable foreign currency drops to around $449 billion. That is roughly where India was in 2021.

The Iran war did not create a reserves problem. It exposed that the real liquid buffer was much thinner than the top-line number

FCNR redux sounds reasonable but the maths is completely different from 2013.

Back then US rates were near zero, so RBI could offer concessional swaps cheaply and still pull in $26 billion.

Today the Fed funds rate is 3.5-3.75%, making every hedged dollar far more expensive for RBI to absorb.
FCNR-B flows have already collapsed 87% this year to under $1 billion.

Even if RBI reopens the window, the cost per dollar raised will eat into its own balance sheet.

Import cover as a metric assumes exports go to zero. The net BoP deficit of $30 billion against $698 billion in reserves gives you a 5% drawdown rate per year, which is manageable in isolation.

The problem is three things hitting at once - crude above $110, FPI outflows crossing $20 billion in 2026, and the rupee already down 5.5% year-to-date.

Each one drains dollars through a different channel simultaneously.


Ira Dugal: India’s central bank is studying ways to boost dollar flows, including the now-famed FCNR route.

While headline level of FX reserves looks adequate, adjusted for gold and forward book, they provide about 7-odd months of import cover.

The thinking is on. Reuters @kalrajs23 and

items.title: Deepinder Goyal says AI is our friend, not a threat - "Some people wonder whether AI chat interfaces will become the new front door for commerce, shif...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal says AI is our friend, not a threat -

"Some people wonder whether AI chat interfaces will become the new front door for commerce, shifting demand away from apps like ours.

We've seen such a story play out before. Google spent over a decade trying to pull transactional behaviour into its own platform. Google Flights. Google Hotels. Google Shopping. Restaurant ordering is built into Google Maps. They had the largest demand surface on earth, billions of searches a day, and the strongest distribution advantage in the history of the world.

And yet Booking dot com is still here. Expedia is still here. Amazon is still here. The vertical apps that consumers had built habits around didn't get displaced.

The reason is simple. General-purpose interfaces are good for general-purpose queries. They are poor interfaces for complex, high-frequency, habitual transactions. Ordering food involves browsing menus, comparing restaurants, ratings, and delivery times, substituting items, and tracking orders in real time.

And more fundamentally, consumer behaviour is the hardest thing in the world to change. People who order dinner on Zomato four times a week, or groceries on Blinkit every other day, are not going to reroute those habits through a chat window. The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI assistants are a real but early segment, and currently, nowhere close to disrupting our business.

Brand associations are even harder to change than habits. When someone is hungry, they think Zomato. When they need groceries within minutes, they think Blinkit. When they want to go out, they think District. This is by design. It is why we run separate apps and focus on creating super brands, rather than a single super app. Each brand owns a specific trigger in the customer's mind. That mental real estate, built over years of consistent delivery on a specific promise, is not something that gets dislodged easily.

Also, most of our business happens in the offline realm. AI will not replace the need to move food through traffic. It will not stock shelves in a dark store. It will not manage crowd flow at a 50,000-person event. It will not build cold chain infrastructure or negotiate with suppliers in tier 2 cities. The physical world doesn't get disrupted by language models.

Nevertheless, if something similar didn't work in the past, it doesn't mean it won't work in the future. Agentic commerce is similar to, and also different from, Google's attempt at owning transactions. At this point, we believe there is nothing to panic about. But we will keep our eyes open, without an iota of complacency or wishful thinking, and edit the contours of our business where needed to make it even more defensible in the long term.

Now, what AI actually does for us –

We are one of the largest deployers of AI in India. Demand prediction, route optimisation, supply-chain management, customer experience, fraud detection, catalogue quality, and partner support already use AI 4 across our platforms. These systems are trained on billions of real interactions, where the cost of getting something wrong — a late delivery, a wrong order, a failed refund, an unavailable item- is immediate and measurable.

But the more important question is not how AI helps us reduce costs. That is useful, but not enough. The sharper question we ask ourselves is: how does AI expand the market?

In our businesses, markets expand when more people can participate — more customers, more delivery partners, more restaurants, more sellers, more brands, and more local businesses. AI reduces the friction required for each of them to participate.

For customers, conversational interfaces let customers express intent in full sentences rather than two-word search queries, which is a far richer signal to convert against. It lowers the friction caused by static search and filters into intent-led discovery, especially for older users, non-native language speakers, first-time online buyers, and tier 2 and 3 city consumers who may struggle with app flows. Zomato’s Healthy Mode and Natural Language Search (NLS) are early examples of this. They could not have been built without AI.

For delivery partners, AI will make onboarding, training, and day-to-day guidance conversational and personalised, thereby expanding the earning opportunity to people who may otherwise struggle with app flows, language, documentation, or lack of confidence.

For restaurant partners and sellers, AI will make high-quality business guidance available from day one — the kind of growth-guidance that historically our teams could provide to a limited set of partners.

AI lets us serve people and businesses that were previously too hard, too small, too fragmented, or too operationally expensive to serve well. Operational efficiency will follow, but it is not the ambition. Our moat remains the physical world. AI makes that moat wider by allowing more of India to participate in it

Src - Deepinder Goyal Q4FY26 Eternal Shareholder Letter, no reco

items.title: Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins. EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), e...
items.description_html:
Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins.

EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), even as:
– Aluminium & metals prices rose ~41%
– Battery and electronics costs remained volatile

What helped:
• Better sourcing & inventory planning
• Use of alternate materials (light rare-earth magnets)
• Shift to LFP batteries
• Product design changes

A good example of how execution can offset cost pressures.

items.title: From the Kotak Institutional Equities - Strategy Note by Sanjeev Prasad, MD & Co-Head. "With no major state election scheduled until early 2027, the g...
items.description_html:
From the Kotak Institutional Equities - Strategy Note by Sanjeev Prasad, MD & Co-Head.

"With no major state election scheduled until early 2027, the government enters a relatively election-free corridor of 10 months.

We expect the government to firmly focus on managing India's weakening macro environment, amid
(1) elevated crude oil prices,
(2) risks to food inflation from a sub-par monsoon and
(3) a widening current account deficit.

In our view, policymakers may focus on
(1) rationalizing energy subsidies, (2) accelerating trade diversification,
(3) finalizing the India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement and
(4) accelerating certain slow-moving reforms."

items.title: RT KV Iyyer - BHARAT 🇮🇳🇮🇱: Late Shri Manohar Parrikar Ji once narrated his ordeal. "I'm from Parra, a village in Goa, so we're called 'Par...
items.description_html:
RT KV Iyyer - BHARAT 🇮🇳🇮🇱
Late Shri Manohar Parrikar Ji once narrated his ordeal.

"I'm from Parra, a village in Goa, so we're called 'Parrikars.' My village is famous for its watermelons. When I was a child, the farmers there held a 'Watermelon Eating Contest' in May, after the harvest. All the children were invited and asked to eat as many watermelons as they wanted.

Many years later, I went to IIT Mumbai to study engineering. Then I returned to my village after 6.5 years. I went to the market to look for watermelons. But they were gone. The ones I found were very small.

I went to meet the farmer who used to hold the 'Watermelon Eating Contest.' Now his son had taken his place. He still held the contest, but there was a difference. When the old farmer offered us watermelons to eat, he would ask us to spit the seeds into a bowl. We were forbidden to chew the seeds. He was collecting seeds for the next crop.

We were, in effect, unpaid child laborers.

He would keep his best watermelons for the competition, using them as the best. He obtained good seeds, which produced even bigger watermelons the next year. When his son arrived, he thought the larger ones would fetch a higher price in the market, so he started selling the larger ones and keeping the smaller ones for competition. The next year, the watermelons grew smaller, and the next even smaller. A watermelon generation lasts one year.

In seven years, Parra's best watermelons were wiped out. In humans, a generation changes every 25 years. In 200 years, we will realize the mistakes we were making in educating our children.

Selecting good seeds, that is, talent, is a huge task in itself. Due to irrelevant ideas and useless things, our good watermelons will go to market, leaving us with useless, inferior seeds.

We must think about this in today's context.

items.title: In FY26, 11 Cr Indians completed transactions worth over $10 billion through Blinkit, District, and Zomato The company Deepinder Goyal accidentally st...
items.description_html:
In FY26, 11 Cr Indians completed transactions worth over $10 billion through Blinkit, District, and Zomato

The company Deepinder Goyal accidentally started, now touches how India eats, shops, and goes out. It took 18 years to get to $10 billion (per year).

This doubling to $20 billion annual NOV will take less than two years from here, as per his guidance!

Similarly, it took 16 years to reach Adjusted EBITDA profitability in FY24. From there, Eternal expects to reach $1 billion of Adjusted EBITDA, hopefully by FY29

The Interesting Part? Zomato started as a restaurant listing website. They spent nine years iterating: reviews, discovery, table reservations, and international expansion. They didn't even start delivering food until 2017. The business that processes billions today didn't exist for the first nine years!

Src - Deepinder Goyal Q4FY25 Letter to Eternal Shareholders, no reco

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Bernstein on Kotak BK Market Perform, TP Rs 500 Kotak closed FY26 on a strong note, with a beat across most operating metrics . NIM ex...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Bernstein on Kotak BK
Market Perform, TP Rs 500
Kotak closed FY26 on a strong note, with a beat across most operating metrics .
NIM expanded ~13 bps QoQ — the highest amongst the top 4 PVBs — while credit costs normalized to 39 bps (-25 bps YoY) on the back of broad-based asset quality improvement.
Loan growth held up at ~16% YoY, and stable opex growth helped offset weaker NOI to drive RoA back above 2%.
RoE at 12.3%, however, remained below the other large PVBs, weighed down by elevated capital buffers.

UBS on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 500
Q4 PAT beat driven by higher PPOP and lower credit cost
Loan/Deposit growth at 3.2%/5.5% QoQ; CASA ratio up 200bp QoQ
Management expect one time ECL transition impact at <2% of net worth
At current valuations (1.5x March 2027E P/BV vs 1.5x - 1.9x for private peers), risk reward looks favourable given expected acceleration in PPOP growth, improvement in asset quality, range bound NIMs & momentum in non-lending business

JPM on Kotak BK
OW, TP Rs 476
delivered a steady 4Q with PAT at Rs 40.3bn (+13% y/y, RoE: 12.1%) coming largely in-line with JPMe.
Core performance was strong with broad-based beats across NII (+1% vs. JPMe), Opex (-3% vs. JPMe) and loan loss provisions (-35% vs JPMe) partially offset by muted non-interest income (-2% y/y, on unwinding of NDF positions and muted card growth).
NII growth of 8.1% y/y (+4.1% q/q) was among the best in the large private bank peer group, with core NIMs (4.54%) holding steady q/q on deposit re-pricing.
Reported NIMs rose by 13bps q/q to 4.67%, on day count benefit in 4Q.

Nomura on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
4Q26: Strong NIM delivery, cautious guidance
Sharp asset quality improvement
Raise FY27-28F EPS by ~2%, led by lower credit costs and opex
Expect RoA/RoE to improve to 2%/12% by FY28F (vs. 1.9%/11% in FY26), with strong EPS CAGR of 18% over FY27-28F

Jefferies on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 450
Q4 standalone profit of Rs40bn (13% YoY) was ahead of est with lower credit costs.
Loans grew by 16% and NII by 8%; growth in CASA was healthy at 16%.
Credit quality is improving with better trends in MFI & credit cards.
Still trim earnings by 3-4% as factor in lower NIM guidance due to higher cost of deposits
Vals are reasonable at 14x FY27E standalone adj. PE.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh
